items.title: Master Admin Role Assignment Restriction
items.description: Master Admin role cannot be assigned through user invite. System prevents selection of Master Admin role during invite creation.

items.title: Invited User Permission Restrictions
items.description: Invited users cannot modify user roles or create new invites unless explicitly granted those permissions. Options are hidden or disabled, and direct API access returns authorization errors.

items.title: Backend Privilege Escalation Prevention
items.description: System blocks privilege escalation attempts at the backend level. Modified requests attempting to escalate roles return 403 Forbidden with no database changes.

items.title: Time-Based Invitation Tokens
items.description: System generates unique access tokens for time-based invitations. Tokens are linked to invited users and are unique in the database.

items.title: Token Time Window Validation
items.description: Tokens allow access only during defined time range. Access denied before start time and after expiration. No grace period unless specified.

items.title: Automatic Access Revocation on Token Expiry
items.description: User sessions are automatically invalidated when tokens expire. Users cannot perform actions and must re-login. Expired tokens cannot be reused.

items.title: Token Time Validation Security
items.description: Token validation is based on server time. Changing client timezone does not extend access. System prevents creation when start time equals end time.

items.title: Overlapping Time-Based Invites Handling
items.description: System correctly handles multiple overlapping time-based invites for same user. No privilege extension beyond defined windows.

items.title: On-Call Shift Detection Before Role Edit
items.description: System detects active or upcoming shifts before allowing role edits. Warning prompt appears when editing roles assigned to on-call users.

items.title: Role Edit Warning Details
items.description: Warning prompt lists impacted users by name with shift status (Active/Upcoming). Explains impact on on-call coverage.

items.title: Role Edit Decision Options
items.description: Cancel is default action. Immediate application requires extra confirmation. Scheduled application defaults to end of last active shift. Options are mutually exclusive.

items.title: Backend Enforcement of Shift Validation
items.description: Backend enforces shift validation. Direct API calls or URL manipulation cannot bypass prompt flow. Returns 403/validation error.

items.title: Role Change Audit Logging
items.description: Audit logs capture admin, timestamp, impacted users, before/after states, effective time, and decision type (Immediate/Scheduled/Cancelled).

items.title: Role Edit Without On-Call Users
items.description: No warning prompt appears when editing roles assigned to users with no active or upcoming shifts. Changes apply immediately.

items.title: Dynamic Shift Status Re-validation
items.description: System re-validates shift status before applying changes. If shifts end during edit flow, restrictions are lifted automatically.

items.title: Mixed Shift Status Handling
items.description: Warning lists only affected users when role has users with different shift statuses (Active, Upcoming, No shift).

items.title: Overlapping Shift Effective Time Calculation
items.description: Default effective time for scheduled changes is end of LAST active shift. No gap miscalculation for overlapping or back-to-back shifts.

items.title: Concurrent Admin Role Edit Handling
items.description: System detects version conflicts when multiple admins edit same role. Proper concurrency handling prevents silent overwrites.

items.title: Scheduled Change Conflict Handling
items.description: System handles conflicts when user gets new shift during scheduled role change period. Follows defined business rules.

items.title: Complex Scenario Audit Accuracy
items.description: Audit logs accurately capture decision type, full impacted users list, timestamps, before/after states, and admin ID for complex scenarios.

items.title: Large User Base Role Edit Performance
items.description: Shift detection and warning display complete within acceptable time for roles assigned to 500+ users. No timeout or partial listing.
